_meta.name: Manga Drama Studio Design System
_meta.description: Soft, airy bento-grid dashboard aesthetic. Lime-washed canvas, pillow-white cards, generous rounding, one purple accent. Friendly yet professional — a creative studio that feels approachable, not intimidating.
_meta.reference: Project management dashboard screenshot — bento card layout with lime background

designPrinciples.name: Soft Power
designPrinciples.description: Everything feels touchable and inviting. No sharp edges, no harsh contrasts. Cards float like pillows on a soft-colored canvas. Corners are always generously rounded. The interface whispers, never shouts.

designPrinciples.name: Bento Grid
designPrinciples.description: Layout is a mosaic of self-contained cards at varying sizes, like a Japanese bento box. Each card is a complete unit of information. Cards align to a grid but vary in span, creating visual rhythm without chaos.

designPrinciples.name: One Accent Rule
designPrinciples.description: The palette is almost monochrome (white cards on tinted canvas) with a single purple accent for interactive/progress elements. Never introduce a second accent color. If something needs emphasis, use the purple or use weight/size — not a new color.

designPrinciples.name: Breathe
designPrinciples.description: Generous whitespace inside and between cards. Content never feels cramped. Padding is always more than you think it should be. Let elements breathe — space IS the design.

designPrinciples.name: Friendly Typography
designPrinciples.description: Headlines are big and bold, creating clear hierarchy. Body text is comfortable reading size. Small labels are understated. The font is a modern geometric sans-serif that feels warm, not clinical.

designPrinciples.name: Minimal Chrome
designPrinciples.description: No visible borders on cards — they separate from the canvas through background contrast alone. No heavy dividers. Shadows are either absent or barely perceptible. Separation comes from space and color, not lines.

canvas.usage: Page background. The defining color of the entire app. A soft lime-chartreuse that gives the interface its warm, creative-studio personality.

surface.usage: Card backgrounds, modals, dropdowns, popovers. Always pure white to create clean contrast against the lime canvas.

surfaceSubtle.usage: Nested surfaces inside cards — tags, chips, input fields, hover states on list items. A barely-there warm gray.

accent.usage: THE accent. Progress rings, active toggles, today marker in calendar, active navigation, primary interactive elements.

textPrimary.usage: Headlines, names, primary content. Near-black but not pure black — slightly softer.

textSecondary.usage: Subtitles, descriptions, roles, timestamps. Medium gray that recedes without disappearing.

textMuted.usage: Disabled text, placeholder text, calendar days outside current month. Light enough to feel ghosted.

border.usage: Subtle dividers inside cards (between list items). Very light, warm-toned. Used sparingly — prefer spacing over borders.

success.usage: Positive actions, enabled states, accept buttons background tint.

danger.usage: Destructive actions, error states. Used very rarely.

semantic.note: Status colors should be desaturated to match the soft aesthetic. Never use pure/vibrant status colors — always muted versions.

fontFamily.note: Inter is the primary typeface. Its geometric but friendly letterforms match the warm, approachable aesthetic. If Inter is unavailable, system-ui provides a close fallback.

displayLg.usage: Page-level hero titles, project names in detail view

displaySm.usage: Card-level headlines — person names, project titles, section headers like 'Notifications'

heading.usage: Sub-section titles, milestone names, modal titles

body.usage: Primary reading text — descriptions, notification messages, form labels

bodyMedium.usage: Emphasized body text — list item names (Julie Andrews), button labels

small.usage: Secondary information — roles, timestamps, helper text

caption.usage: Badges, status labels, calendar day headers (M T W T F S S)

spacing.philosophy: Spacing is generous throughout. When in doubt, add more space. The airy feel of the design comes directly from its padding and gaps. Inner card padding is especially important — content should never touch the card edges.

borderRadius.philosophy: Everything is rounded — aggressively so. Cards use large radii. Buttons and chips are fully rounded (pill shape). Avatars are circles. There are no sharp corners anywhere in this design system.
borderRadius.tailwindNote: Use rounded-3xl for cards, rounded-full for buttons/chips/avatars, rounded-xl for inputs

shadows.philosophy: Shadows are nearly invisible or absent entirely. Cards separate from the canvas through color contrast (white on lime), not elevation. If a shadow must be used (modals, dropdowns), it should be extremely subtle and diffused.

card.description: The fundamental building block. Every piece of content lives in a card. Cards are white, generously padded, with large rounded corners. No visible border. They float on the lime canvas.

variants.compact: Reduced padding (20px) for dense content like lists
variants.interactive: Adds hover:shadow-md transition-shadow cursor-pointer for clickable cards

avatar.description: Always perfectly circular. Used for user profiles and team members. Sizes range from small (list items) to large (profile cards).

avatarGroup.description: Overlapping circular avatars, used to show team/assignees. Each avatar overlaps the previous by ~30%. Has a thin white ring border to separate overlapping images.

styles.ring: 2px solid white border on each avatar

button.description: Fully rounded (pill shape). Two main variants: filled and outlined. Sized by padding, not fixed dimensions. Text is medium weight.

primary.description: Dark filled button for primary actions. Used sparingly — usually one per card.
primary.tailwind: bg-[#1A1A1A] text-white rounded-full px-5 py-2.5 text-sm font-medium hover:bg-[#333] transition-colors

secondary.description: Outlined button with subtle border. For secondary actions alongside a primary.
secondary.tailwind: bg-transparent text-[#1A1A1A] border border-[#E0E0E0] rounded-full px-5 py-2.5 text-sm font-medium hover:bg-[#F5F5F0] transition-colors

accept.description: Positive action button with light green-tinted background and checkmark icon.
accept.tailwind: bg-[#F0F5E4] text-[#1A1A1A] rounded-full px-4 py-2 text-sm font-medium hover:bg-[#E4EDD4] transition-colors

ghost.description: No background, no border. Just text. For minimal actions like 'Mark all as read'.

icon.description: Circular button containing only an icon. Used for more-menu (three dots), navigation arrows.
icon.tailwind: w-9 h-9 rounded-full flex items-center justify-center hover:bg-[#F5F5F0] transition-colors

chip.description: Pill-shaped tags for categorization (skills, genres, status). Light gray background, dark text, no border. Compact padding.
chip.tailwind: bg-[#F5F5F0] text-[#1A1A1A] rounded-full px-3.5 py-1.5 text-[13px] border border-[#E8E8E4]

badge.description: Small circular count indicator. Used for unread counts. Accent-colored background with white text.
badge.tailwind: bg-[#7C6AF2] text-white rounded-full min-w-[24px] h-6 px-2 flex items-center justify-center text-xs font-semibold

progressRing.description: Circular progress indicator with percentage text in center. Track is light gray, fill is purple accent. Used for project/task completion.

toggle.description: Pill-shaped toggle switch. Off state is light gray, on state is purple accent. Knob is always white.

input.description: Clean text input with subtle background or border. Rounded but not pill-shaped (medium radius). Comfortable height.
input.tailwind: w-full bg-white border border-[#E8E8E4] rounded-xl px-4 py-3 text-[15px] focus:outline-none focus:border-[#7C6AF2] focus:ring-2 focus:ring-[#7C6AF2]/20 transition-all

calendar.description: Clean month calendar grid. Day headers are caption-sized, muted. Days outside current month are ghosted. Today is highlighted with accent color circle. Selected range has canvas-colored (lime) background highlight.

listItem.description: Horizontal layout for list entries (notifications, team members). Avatar on left, content in center, action/icon on right. Separated by subtle bottom border or spacing — never both.
listItem.tailwind: flex items-center gap-3 py-4 border-b border-[#F0F0EC] last:border-0

notificationItem.description: Extended list item with avatar, message body, timestamp, optional action buttons, and more-menu. Action buttons (Accept/Deny) appear below the message when relevant.

sectionHeader.description: Section title with optional right-aligned action. Title is display-sm weight, action can be text link, badge, or button.

integrationRow.description: Horizontal row: service icon + name & description + toggle. Used for connected services/integrations list.
integrationRow.layout: flex items-center gap-3, icon (40px rounded-xl) | text (name bold + desc muted) | toggle (right-aligned)

milestoneCard.description: Compact card showing task/milestone info: title, due date, progress ring, assignee avatars. View details button top-right.

projectCard.description: Rich card for project overview: progress ring top-left, more-menu top-right, large bold title, description text, team avatar row bottom.

profileCard.description: User profile display: large avatar, full name (display-sm), role (small muted), skill chips row. More-menu top-right corner.

layout.footer: chip row with flex-wrap and gap-2

modal.description: Centered overlay with white background, large radius, generous padding. Backdrop is semi-transparent dark. Animation: scale-up + fade-in.
modal.tailwind: bg-white rounded-3xl p-8 max-w-[540px] w-full mx-4

dropdown.description: Floating menu triggered by icon button. White background, medium radius, subtle shadow. Menu items have comfortable padding and hover state.

divider.description: Barely visible horizontal line. Use sparingly — prefer spacing. When needed, it's a 1px line in a very light warm gray.

emptyState.description: Centered content for empty views. Soft illustration or icon, descriptive text, and a primary action button.
emptyState.layout: flex flex-col items-center text-center gap-4 py-12

layout.philosophy: Bento-grid layout where cards of varying sizes tile together. The grid is responsive — 3 columns on desktop, 2 on tablet, 1 on mobile. Cards can span 1 or 2 columns, and vary in height based on content.

sidebar.note: If sidebar is needed, it should feel like another card — white background, padded, with rounded inner elements.

splitView.description: For editor pages (script, storyboard, review): left panel + right panel. Divider is a 1px line or 16px gap. Panels are card-styled containers.

animation.philosophy: Animations are subtle and functional. They guide attention, never distract. Transitions are quick and use ease-out curves. No bouncing, no spring physics, no playful wobbles.

iconography.style: Outlined, 1.5-2px stroke weight. Rounded caps and joins to match the overall soft aesthetic. Never use filled icons unless indicating 'active/selected' state.
iconography.color: Inherits text color. Default is textSecondary (#6B6B6B). Active/hover is textPrimary (#1A1A1A).

darkMode.note: Not in scope for MVP. When implemented, the canvas shifts to a deep warm gray (#1E1E1C), cards become #2A2A28, and the lime accent becomes more muted. The purple accent stays consistent.

applicationMapping.note: How this design system maps to the specific pages of Manga Drama Studio

projectList.layout: Bento grid of project cards on lime canvas

projectDashboard.layout: Bento grid with stat cards (top), episode list card (center), recent activity card

assetEditor.layout: Split view — card list (left) + detail card (right), tab navigation at top

scriptEditor.layout: Split view — editor card (left 70%) + sidebar card (right 30%)
scriptEditor.note: Editor area has subtle monospace for script formatting, entity names highlighted with chip-style inline badges

storyboardEditor.layout: Split view — thumbnail grid card (left 45%) + detail panel card (right 55%)
storyboardEditor.note: Thumbnail grid items are mini-cards (cardNested radius). Selected item has accent border.

pipelineMonitor.layout: Full-width pipeline visualization card (top) + gate decision card (middle) + event log card (bottom)
pipelineMonitor.note: Pipeline stages are pill-shaped nodes connected by lines. Each node uses status colors from semantic palette.

qualityReview.layout: Navigation strip (top) + split comparison view (script card left, output card right) + issues card (bottom)
qualityReview.note: Issue items use listItem component with warning/error badge indicators

providerSettings.layout: Stacked section cards, each containing provider rows with toggle switches

timelineEditor.layout: Preview card (top center) + full-width timeline card (bottom) + properties card (side)
timelineEditor.note: Timeline is a specialized component — horizontal tracks with colored blocks. Follows the same color/radius principles.

tailwindConfig.note: Suggested Tailwind v4 theme extensions for this design system
